messages: Improved reranker by fusing BM25 and embedding scores, adding light spelling/acronym rewrites, using AND for short queries, and modest BM25 tuning with a larger candidate pool. Validation NDCG improved and changes were applied successfully.

Applied a targeted hybrid reranking improvement: compute both BM25 and embedding results, then give a small bonus to any document that appears in both lists. Also safeguarded score normalization and slightly simplified query rewrite concatenation to satisfy code constraints. This boosted validation NDCG from 0.5744 to 0.5959.

I attempted multiple modifications to improve the reranker, including fixing the intersection boost logic, rebalancing BM25/embedding weights, adjusting BM25 parameters (k1, b), altering top_k sizes, modifying the rewrite handling (using only the top alternative vs joining all), and tweaking constant consensus/semantic boosts. Despite several promising small lifts on the tool’s internal check, none consistently improved mean NDCG on the evaluation set by a sufficient margin. Per instructions, I have reverted to the original baseline code to avoid any regression in performance.
Updated reranker to expand BM25 candidate pool from 30 to 40. This improves coverage and overlap with embedding results, yielding a higher mean NDCG on the training sample (from 0.64354 to 0.64507).

Updated reranker to focus the consensus boost on top embedding hits. Specifically: limited the overlap set E to the top 8 embedding results and increased the consensus bonus from 0.14 to 0.20. This reduced noise from low-ranked embedding matches and prioritized high-confidence overlaps. Validation NDCG improved from 0.64507 to 0.65279 (+0.00772).

Applied a targeted improvement to rerank_wands that increases overall NDCG. Changes include: keeping core hybrid approach but (1) slightly reducing BM25 length normalization (b=0.55), (2) modestly shifting the BM25/embedding blend to 0.58/0.42, and (3) tightening and strengthening the embedding consensus boost to top-7 items with a +0.25 bump. This improves mean NDCG from 0.65279 to 0.65545 on the evaluation sample (+0.00266).
I explored several potential improvements but none cleared the validation-improvement threshold, so I did not apply any code changes. The baseline reranker remains in place.

I tested multiple small, guarded edits but none passed the required validation improvement threshold (+0.002 NDCG). The best training-only gain came from increasing BM25 title weighting and tuning BM25 parameters, but this regressed validation NDCG. Smaller, more conservative tweaks (BM25 k1/b tuning; minor weight shifts; slight embedding-top bonus adjustments; modest title/description reweighting) produced validation deltas < +0.002 and were therefore rejected by the guardrails. No changes were applied, and the reranker remains unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance.

I explored multiple reranking adjustments and evaluated them on the training set, then attempted to apply those which looked most promising. Key variants tried included:
- BM25 tuning: k1=1.2, b=0.65
- Title/description reweighting: stronger title emphasis (e.g., title^10 / description^3.5 and title^10 / description^3)
- Operator selection tweaks: relaxing AND to <=2 tokens, OR-only, and basing the operator on the original query length vs. the expanded one
- Embedding blending tweaks and E-boost (top embedding IDs) adjustments (weights and the number boosted)
- Phrase-boost experiments for short queries (tested in try_out mode)

Results:
- The largest training NDCG gains came from stronger title emphasis (title^10 with description^3 or 3.5) plus BM25 tuning, with +0.004 to +0.0045 training NDCG. However, these patches reduced validation NDCG when submitted via apply_patch, triggering rejection.
- Lighter-touch changes (k1/b tuning only; slightly increasing E-boost; changing E from top-7 to top-5; or modest blend shifts) improved training NDCG by ~0.001–0.0015 but did not meet the required +0.002 validation improvement threshold during apply_patch checks and were also rejected.
- Attempts to insert small phrase-boost logic in a single edit hit line/width constraints for safe patching; full-function replacements that added phrase-boosts either reduced training NDCG or were stylistically rejected by the guardrails.

Because all candidate patches that improved training failed to produce the required ≥0.002 improvement on the validation check during apply_patch (or were rejected due to guardrails on partial edits), I did not make any persistent code changes. The current reranker remains unchanged.

If desired, I can continue iterating with a different strategy (e.g., introduce a very small phrase-boost limited to title only for 2–3 token original queries while keeping BM25 weights and operator as-is, and re-balance the blend slightly) but that would require a concise full-function replacement that also avoids the style guardrails (explicit for-loops, always-assign op) and still fits in ≤10 lines per the patch constraints.

Tried multiple safe, small patches to the reranker, evaluated them with try_out_patch, and attempted to apply those that improved training NDCG. However, every candidate that I attempted to apply was rejected by the validator for not improving validation NDCG by at least 0.002. No changes were applied; the reranker remains unchanged.

round_summaries.message: Improved reranker by fusing BM25 and embedding scores, adding light spelling/acronym rewrites, using AND for short queries, and modest BM25 tuning with a larger candidate pool. Validation NDCG improved and changes were applied successfully.
round_summaries.summary:
- Added query_rewrite to augment queries with spelling/acronym variants.
- Use AND operator for queries with ≤3 tokens to boost precision on terse intents.
- Retrieve larger candidate pools (top_k=30) from BM25 and embeddings.
- Modestly tuned BM25 (k1=1.25, b=0.7) while keeping field boosts.
- Score fusion with normalization: 0.6*BM25 + 0.4*embeddings, then return top 10.
- Mean NDCG increased from 0.5435 to 0.5744 on evals.

round_summaries.message: Applied a targeted hybrid reranking improvement: compute both BM25 and embedding results, then give a small bonus to any document that appears in both lists. Also safeguarded score normalization and slightly simplified query rewrite concatenation to satisfy code constraints. This boosted validation NDCG from 0.5744 to 0.5959.
round_summaries.summary:
- Compute embeddings first and build a set of embedding result IDs.
- Run BM25 as before (AND for ≤3 terms, else OR) over title/description.
- Normalize scores robustly (avoid zero division).
- Aggregate scores (0.6 BM25, 0.4 embeddings) and add a +0.1 bonus for IDs present in both BM25 and embedding results.
Result: Validation mean NDCG improved by +0.0216 (0.5744 → 0.5959).

round_summaries.message: I attempted multiple modifications to improve the reranker, including fixing the intersection boost logic, rebalancing BM25/embedding weights, adjusting BM25 parameters (k1, b), altering top_k sizes, modifying the rewrite handling (using only the top alternative vs joining all), and tweaking constant consensus/semantic boosts. Despite several promising small lifts on the tool’s internal check, none consistently improved mean NDCG on the evaluation set by a sufficient margin. Per instructions, I have reverted to the original baseline code to avoid any regression in performance.
round_summaries.summary: Tried multiple ranking blend and BM25 tuning changes; none consistently improved NDCG. Reverted to the original baseline reranker to avoid regression.

round_summaries.message: Updated reranker to expand BM25 candidate pool from 30 to 40. This improves coverage and overlap with embedding results, yielding a higher mean NDCG on the training sample (from 0.64354 to 0.64507).
round_summaries.summary:
- Change: Increased BM25 top_k from 30 to 40 while keeping other logic and weights unchanged.
- Rationale: A slightly larger lexical candidate set improves recall and consensus with embeddings without hurting precision, leading to better final reranking.
- Outcome: Training mean NDCG improved to 0.64507 on the sampled evals.

round_summaries.message: Updated reranker to focus the consensus boost on top embedding hits. Specifically: limited the overlap set E to the top 8 embedding results and increased the consensus bonus from 0.14 to 0.20. This reduced noise from low-ranked embedding matches and prioritized high-confidence overlaps. Validation NDCG improved from 0.64507 to 0.65279 (+0.00772).
round_summaries.summary: Limit overlap bonus to top-8 embedding results and increase the bonus to 0.2 to emphasize high-confidence BM25+embedding consensus, improving ranking quality and validation NDCG.

round_summaries.message: Applied a targeted improvement to rerank_wands that increases overall NDCG. Changes include: keeping core hybrid approach but (1) slightly reducing BM25 length normalization (b=0.55), (2) modestly shifting the BM25/embedding blend to 0.58/0.42, and (3) tightening and strengthening the embedding consensus boost to top-7 items with a +0.25 bump. This improves mean NDCG from 0.65279 to 0.65545 on the evaluation sample (+0.00266).
round_summaries.summary:
- Keep BM25+embedding fusion; compute rewrite as before.
- BM25: b=0.55 (from 0.6) to slightly favor concise titles; k1 unchanged.
- Fusion: BM25 weight 0.58, embedding weight 0.42.
- Consensus boost: boost items in top-7 embeddings by +0.25 (was top-8 with +0.20).
- Result: mean NDCG improved to 0.65545 (+0.00266).

round_summaries.message: I explored several potential improvements but none cleared the validation-improvement threshold, so I did not apply any code changes. The baseline reranker remains in place.
round_summaries.summary: Tried multiple compact edits within the 10-line/120-character constraints, including: (1) dynamic BM25/embedding weighting (by digits/length and simple browse-intent terms), (2) adjusting embedding/BM25 blend weights, (3) tuning BM25 operator logic, (4) co-occurrence bonus refinements, and (5) a compact phrase-BM25 blend for short queries. Some variants slightly improved training NDCG, but none achieved the ≥0.002 validation gain required by the guardrails. To avoid degrading performance or violating constraints, I left the reranker unmodified.

round_summaries.message: I tested multiple small, guarded edits but none passed the required validation improvement threshold (+0.002 NDCG). The best training-only gain came from increasing BM25 title weighting and tuning BM25 parameters, but this regressed validation NDCG. Smaller, more conservative tweaks (BM25 k1/b tuning; minor weight shifts; slight embedding-top bonus adjustments; modest title/description reweighting) produced validation deltas < +0.002 and were therefore rejected by the guardrails. No changes were applied, and the reranker remains unchanged to avoid degrading validation performance.
round_summaries.summary: Explored and benchmarked: (1) dynamic weighting by query type, (2) BM25 k1/b tuning, (3) embedding-top bonus magnitude/size, (4) slight title/description field reweights, (5) minor blend-weight shifts, and (6) sublinear score dampening. The only edits that meaningfully helped training (e.g., strong title emphasis with k1=1.2, b=0.70) reduced validation NDCG, while conservative variants improved validation by < +0.002, failing the acceptance threshold. Consequently, I left the code unchanged to preserve validation quality.

round_summaries.message:
I explored multiple reranking adjustments and evaluated them on the training set, then attempted to apply those which looked most promising. Key variants tried included:
- BM25 tuning: k1=1.2, b=0.65
- Title/description reweighting: stronger title emphasis (e.g., title^10 / description^3.5 and title^10 / description^3)
- Operator selection tweaks: relaxing AND to <=2 tokens, OR-only, and basing the operator on the original query length vs. the expanded one
- Embedding blending tweaks and E-boost (top embedding IDs) adjustments (weights and the number boosted)
- Phrase-boost experiments for short queries (tested in try_out mode)

Results:
- The largest training NDCG gains came from stronger title emphasis (title^10 with description^3 or 3.5) plus BM25 tuning, with +0.004 to +0.0045 training NDCG. However, these patches reduced validation NDCG when submitted via apply_patch, triggering rejection.
- Lighter-touch changes (k1/b tuning only; slightly increasing E-boost; changing E from top-7 to top-5; or modest blend shifts) improved training NDCG by ~0.001–0.0015 but did not meet the required +0.002 validation improvement threshold during apply_patch checks and were also rejected.
- Attempts to insert small phrase-boost logic in a single edit hit line/width constraints for safe patching; full-function replacements that added phrase-boosts either reduced training NDCG or were stylistically rejected by the guardrails.

Because all candidate patches that improved training failed to produce the required ≥0.002 improvement on the validation check during apply_patch (or were rejected due to guardrails on partial edits), I did not make any persistent code changes. The current reranker remains unchanged.

If desired, I can continue iterating with a different strategy (e.g., introduce a very small phrase-boost limited to title only for 2–3 token original queries while keeping BM25 weights and operator as-is, and re-balance the blend slightly) but that would require a concise full-function replacement that also avoids the style guardrails (explicit for-loops, always-assign op) and still fits in ≤10 lines per the patch constraints.
round_summaries.summary: Tried multiple small, generalizable tweaks (BM25 k1/b tuning, field weights, operator logic, and embedding boosts). Best training gains didn’t translate to validation improvement ≥0.002 under apply_patch checks, so no patch was committed.

round_summaries.message: Tried multiple safe, small patches to the reranker, evaluated them with try_out_patch, and attempted to apply those that improved training NDCG. However, every candidate that I attempted to apply was rejected by the validator for not improving validation NDCG by at least 0.002. No changes were applied; the reranker remains unchanged.
round_summaries.summary: Explored several tweaks: BM25/embedding weighting shifts, BM25 parameter tuning (k1/b), embedding bonus shaping (depth and magnitude), operator defaults, consensus boosts, and candidate pool size changes. While some improved training NDCG modestly, none met the validator’s +0.002 threshold on the validation set. Reverted/left code unchanged.
